title: Publisher Admission, Buffering, and Backpressure · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release board compares near-miss Go and RabbitMQ decisions for publisher admission, buffering, and backpressure and accepts only bounded ownership, causal evidence, and an explicit live-broker transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a cancellable Go publisher admission controller. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Treat a nil PublishWithContext error as local call acceptance only and separately track flow control, routing, confirmation, and stakeholder effect.
Pause admission after false, coordinate a bounded drain waiter, handle close and abort, and avoid lost wakeups or listener leaks; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Propagate publisher capacity to HTTP or job admission with queue, byte, time, tenant, and rejection budgets; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Publish Context Admission · changed case · Quiz Publish Context Admission
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 2073 during consumer shutdown drill with one blocked handler goroutine. Build deterministic Go evidence to propagate broker blocking, byte capacity, and confirm-window saturation into admission; name the owner and stop path for every goroutine and notification channel; then raise a broker resource alarm and halt new admission. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat a nil PublishWithContext error as local call acceptance only and separately track flow control, routing, confirmation, and stakeholder effect.

Which action demonstrates Publish Context Admission without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Publish Context Admission · changed case · Quiz Publish Context Admission.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for treat a nil publishwithcontext error as local call acceptance only and separately track flow control, routing, confirmation, and stakeholder effect. steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Blocked Unblocked Flow
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Blocked Unblocked Flow: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 3692 during table-driven topology review for a tenant migration. Build deterministic Go evidence to propagate broker blocking, byte capacity, and confirm-window saturation into admission; prove cleanup under a single deadline and cancellation cause; then cancel the parent context while a result is still ambiguous. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Respond to blocked and unblocked connection events, broker flow control, alarms, and local drain evidence without confusing their scopes; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Blocked Unblocked Flow.
steps.why: Retrieving Blocked Unblocked Flow through the concrete a cancellable Go publisher admission controller case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Blocked Unblocked Flow, keep Blocked Unblocked Flow connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Respond to blocked and unblocked connection events, broker flow control, alarms, and local drain evidence without confusing their scopes; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Blocked Unblocked Flow, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Blocked Unblocked Flow precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Blocked Unblocked Flow, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A quiz Go messaging team handles case 3692 during table-driven topology review for a tenant migration. Build deterministic Go evidence to propagate broker blocking, byte capacity, and confirm-window saturation into admission; prove cleanup under a single deadline and cancellation cause; then cancel the parent context while a result is still ambiguous. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Respond to blocked and unblocked connection events, broker flow control, alarms, and local drain evidence without confusing their scopes; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Blocked Unblocked Flow, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A drain event proves RabbitMQ resource alarms and queue backlog have cleared. In Go, PublishWithContext is not proof that broker I/O stopped when its context ended.
